title: Corrective fix for B-1ae8b645-test: Test Update planner template with bead sizing and shared followup guidance
description:
Blocked reason: `tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children` fails because the scheduler still creates `B-9881ba3d-test`, `B-9881ba3d-docs`, and `B-9881ba3d-review` despite existing planner-owned shared follow-up beads.

Remaining work: Scheduler logic still creates legacy per-developer `-test`, `-docs`, and `-review` children for a developer bead even when planner-owned shared follow-up beads already exist at the feature root. That implementation defect must be fixed, then `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator -v` should be rerun.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-1ae8b645-test.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated scheduler followup selection in `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` to only treat feature-root children as planner-owned shared followups. Added a regression test in `tests/test_orchestrator.py` covering shared-root vs nested followup selection. Verified with `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` and `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py tests/test_orchestrator.py`.
handoff_summary.remaining: No code work remains in this corrective bead. Retry parent bead `B-1ae8b645-test` so the tester agent can rerun its `tests.test_orchestrator` validation step.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. This narrows planner-owned followup matching to feature-root-owned shared beads; if any workflow intentionally relied on nested followups being reused as shared planner-owned followups, it will now fall back to legacy lookup instead.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry bead `B-1ae8b645-test` and rerun the orchestrated `tests.test_orchestrator` validation through the tester agent.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. Only `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` changed; downstream agents should preserve the tighter feature-root shared-followup matching when editing adjacent scheduler logic.

conflict_risks: Low. Only `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` changed; downstream agents should preserve the tighter feature-root shared-followup matching when editing adjacent scheduler logic.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-422d9d91-corrective
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-422d9d91
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-d4ab70d3
Restricted planner-owned followup reuse to feature-root shared beads and added a regression test so nested followups cannot shadow shared tester beads.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
